Name: Yanma
Flavor-Text: If it flaps its wings really fast, it can generate shock waves that will shatter windows in the area.

Name: Yanmega
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This six-legged Pokémon is easily capable of transporting an adult in flight. The wings on its tail help it stay balanced.

Name: Celebi
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may look at the top 6 cards of your deck, reveal an Energy card you find there, and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Flavor-Text: It has the power to travel across time, but it is said to appear only in peaceful times.

Name: Cacnea
Flavor-Text: It prefers harsh environments such as deserts. It can survive for 30 days on water stored in its body.

Name: Tropius
Effects: Attach up to 2 [G] Energy cards from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: Bunches of delicious fruit grow around its neck. In warm areas, many ranches raise Tropius.

Name: Rowlet
Ability-Effect: If you played Bird Keeper from your hand during this turn, ignore all Energy in this Pokémon’s attack costs.
Effects: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: At a distance, it launches its sharp feathers while flying about. If the enemy gets too close, Rowlet switches tactics and delivers vicious kicks.

Name: Dartrix
Flavor-Text: It throws one knifelike feather after another at its enemies, and each one precisely strikes a weak point. These feathers are known as blade quills.

Name: Decidueye
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon V and Pokémon-GX.
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: As if wielding a bow, it launches the arrow quills hidden among the feathers of its wings. Decidueye’s shots never miss.

Name: Dhelmise V
Effects: If any of your [G] Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack during their last turn, this attack does 90 more damage.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Giga Hammer.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Dhelmise VMAX
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon for each [G] Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Max Anchor.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Grookey
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: When it uses its special stick to strike up a beat, the sound waves produced carry revitalizing energy to the plants and flowers in the area.

Name: Thwackey
Ability-Effect: If you have a Stadium in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: The faster a Thwackey can beat out a rhythm with its two sticks, the more respect it wins from its peers.

Name: Rillaboom
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 [G] Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: By drumming, it taps into the power of its special tree stump. The roots of the stump follow its direction in battle.

Name: Gossifleur
Flavor-Text: It whirls around in the wind while singing a joyous song. This delightful display has charmed many into raising this Pokémon.

Name: Eldegoss
Effects: Heal all damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: The cotton on the head of this Pokémon can be spun into a glossy, gorgeous yarn—a Galar regional specialty.

Name: Zarude
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Once the vines on Zarude’s body tear off, they become nutrients in the soil. This helps the plants of the forest grow.

Name: Reshiram
Effects: This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: When Reshiram’s tail flares, the heat energy moves the atmosphere and changes the world’s weather.

Name: Cinderace V
Ability-Effect: If a Stadium is in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Cinderace VMAX
Effects: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent’s last turn, this attack does that much more damage.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Horsea
Flavor-Text: It makes its nest in the shade of corals. If it senses danger, it spits murky ink and flees.

Name: Kyogre
Effects: This attack does 80 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It is said to have widened the seas by causing downpours. It had been asleep in a marine trench.

Name: Buizel
Flavor-Text: It inflates the flotation sac around its neck and pokes its head out of the water to see what is going on.

Name: Floatzel
Flavor-Text: Its flotation sac developed as a result of pursuing aquatic prey. It can double as a rubber raft.

Name: Manaphy
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may look at the top 6 cards of your deck, reveal a Pokémon you find there, and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Flavor-Text: It starts its life with a wondrous power that permits it to bond with any kind of Pokémon.

Name: Volcanion
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 120 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It expels its internal steam from the arms on its back. It has enough power to blow away a mountain.

Name: Chewtle
Flavor-Text: It starts off battles by attacking with its rock-hard horn, but as soon as the opponent flinches, this Pokémon bites down and never lets go.

Name: Drednaw
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon rapidly extends its retractable neck to sink its sharp fangs into distant enemies and take them down.

Name: Cramorant
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: This hungry Pokémon swallows Arrokuda whole. Occasionally, it makes a mistake and tries to swallow a Pokémon other than its preferred prey.

Name: Snom
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It spits out thread imbued with a frigid sort of energy and uses it to tie its body to branches, disguising itself as an icicle while it sleeps.

Name: Frosmoth
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a [W] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched [W] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It shows no mercy to any who desecrate fields and mountains. It will fly around on its icy wings, causing a blizzard to chase offenders away.

Name: Shinx
Effects: If your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.

Name: Luxio
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if you drew this Pokémon from your deck at the beginning of your turn and your Bench isn’t full, before you put it into your hand, you may put it onto your Bench.
Flavor-Text: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

Name: Luxray
Effects: If this Pokémon evolved from Luxio during this turn, this attack does 100 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Luxray’s ability to see through objects comes in handy when it’s scouting for danger.

Name: Rotom
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 Item cards that have the word “Rotom” in their name, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: One boy’s invention led to the development of many different machines that take advantage of Rotom’s unique capabilities.

Name: Morpeko
Effects: Draw a card.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: As it eats the seeds stored up in its pocket-like pouches, this Pokémon is not just satisfying its constant hunger. It’s also generating electricity.

Name: Morpeko
Effects: Draw a card.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: As it eats the seeds stored up in its pocket-like pouches, this Pokémon is not just satisfying its constant hunger. It’s also generating electricity.

Name: Morpeko V
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, switch it with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Morpeko VMAX
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Indeedee V
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 60 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Trapinch
Effects: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its nest is a sloped, bowl-like pit in the desert. Once something has fallen in, there is no escape.

Name: Koffing
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its body is full of poisonous gas. It floats into garbage dumps, seeking out the fumes of raw, rotting trash.

Name: Galarian Weezing
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent’s Pokémon in play have no Abilities, except for Neutralizing Gas.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 4 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon during Pokémon Checkup.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon consumes particles that contaminate the air. Instead of leaving droppings, it expels clean air.

Name: Spinarak
Flavor-Text: Although the poison from its fangs isn’t that strong, it’s potent enough to weaken prey that gets caught in its web.

Name: Crobat V
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand. You can’t use more than 1 Dark Asset Ability each turn.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Crobat VMAX
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Yveltal
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Flavor-Text: When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon once more.

Name: Nickit
Effects: Your opponent shuffles their hand and puts it on the bottom of their deck. If they put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, they draw 3 cards.
Flavor-Text: Aided by the soft pads on its feet, it silently raids the food stores of other Pokémon. It survives off its ill-gotten gains.

Name: Thievul
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: It secretly marks potential targets with a scent. By following the scent, it stalks its targets and steals from them when they least expect it.

Name: Cufant
Flavor-Text: It digs up the ground with its trunk. It’s also very strong, being able to carry loads of over five tons without any problem at all.

Name: Ditto V
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may choose a Basic Pokémon V from your discard pile and switch it with this Pokémon. Any attached cards, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.
Effects: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Ditto VMAX
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks and use it as this attack.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Eevee
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from Eevee, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It has the ability to alter the composition of its body to suit its surrounding environment.

Name: Greedent V
Effects: Discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Cramorant V
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 160 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Cramorant VMAX
Effects: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Indeedee
Effects: Search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It uses the horns on its head to sense the emotions of others. Males will act as valets for those they serve, looking after their every need.

Name: Ball Guy
Description: Search your deck for up to 3 different Item cards that have the word “Ball” in their name, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Boss’s Orders
Description: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Gym Trainer
Description: Draw 2 cards. If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent’s last turn, draw 2 more cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Professor’s Research
Description: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Rusted Shield
Description: The Zamazenta V this card is attached to gets +70 HP.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Rusted Sword
Description: The attacks of the Zacian V this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Team Yell Towel
Description: Heal 50 damage from both Active Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Alcremie V
Effects: Heal 30 damage from each of your Benched Pokémon.
If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Ball Guy
Description: Search your deck for up to 3 different Item cards that have the word “Ball” in their name, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Bird Keeper
Description: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, draw 3 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Cara Liss
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 Rare Fossil cards and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Gym Trainer
Description: Draw 2 cards. If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent’s last turn, draw 2 more cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Piers
Description: Search your deck for an Energy card and a [D] Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Poké Kid
Description: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Rose
Description: Attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon VMAX. If you attached any Energy cards in this way, discard your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Skyla
Description: Search your deck for a Trainer card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Alcremie VMAX
Effects: For each of your Benched Pokémon, search your deck for a [P] Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Discard any amount of Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Rowlet
Ability-Effect: If you played Bird Keeper from your hand during this turn, ignore all Energy in this Pokémon’s attack costs.
Effects: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: At a distance, it launches its sharp feathers while flying about. If the enemy gets too close, Rowlet switches tactics and delivers vicious kicks.

Name: Dartrix
Flavor-Text: It throws one knifelike feather after another at its enemies, and each one precisely strikes a weak point. These feathers are known as blade quills.

Name: Decidueye
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon V and Pokémon-GX.
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: As if wielding a bow, it launches the arrow quills hidden among the feathers of its wings. Decidueye’s shots never miss.

Name: Grookey
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: When it uses its special stick to strike up a beat, the sound waves produced carry revitalizing energy to the plants and flowers in the area.

Name: Thwackey
Ability-Effect: If you have a Stadium in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: The faster a Thwackey can beat out a rhythm with its two sticks, the more respect it wins from its peers.

Name: Rillaboom
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 [G] Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: By drumming, it taps into the power of its special tree stump. The roots of the stump follow its direction in battle.

Name: Blipbug
Flavor-Text: Often found in gardens, this Pokémon has hairs on its body that it uses to assess its surroundings.

Name: Dottler
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 40 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: As it grows inside its shell, it uses its psychic abilities to monitor the outside world and prepare for evolution.

Name: Orbeetle
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may look at the top 3 cards of your opponent’s deck and put them back in any order.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each [P] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It emits psychic energy to observe and study what’s around it—and what’s around it can include things over six miles away.

Name: Gossifleur
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It whirls around in the wind while singing a joyous song. This delightful display has charmed many into raising this Pokémon.

Name: Eldegoss
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 [G] Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: The cotton on the head of this Pokémon can be spun into a glossy, gorgeous yarn—a Galar regional specialty.

Name: Applin
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.
Flavor-Text: It spends its entire life inside an apple. It hides from its natural enemies, bird Pokémon, by pretending it’s just an apple and nothing more.

Name: Flapple
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. If you placed any damage counters in this way, shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It ate a sour apple, and that induced its evolution. In its cheeks, it stores an acid capable of causing chemical burns.

Name: Appletun
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Basic Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Eating a sweet apple caused its evolution. A nectarous scent wafts from its body, luring in the bug Pokémon it preys on.

Name: Scorbunny
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has special pads on the backs of its feet, and one on its nose. Once it’s raring to fight, these pads radiate tremendous heat.

Name: Raboot
Effects: Search your deck for a [R] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It kicks berries right off the branches of trees and then juggles them with its feet, practicing its footwork.

Name: Cinderace
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when this Pokémon moves from your Bench to the Active Spot, you may attach up to 2 [R] Energy cards from your discard pile to it.
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s skilled at both offense and defense, and it gets pumped up when cheered on. But if it starts showboating, it could put itself in a tough spot.

Name: Sizzlipede
Flavor-Text: It stores flammable gas in its body and uses it to generate heat. The yellow sections on its belly get particularly hot.

Name: Centiskorch
Effects: For each [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon, discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: When it heats up, its body temperature reaches about 1,500 degrees Fahrenheit. It lashes its body like a whip and launches itself at enemies.

Name: Galarian Mr. Mime
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Its talent is tap-dancing. It can also manipulate temperatures to create a floor of ice, which this Pokémon can kick up to use as a barrier.

Name: Galarian Mr. Rime
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may switch 1 of your opponent’s face-down Prize cards with the top card of their deck. (The cards stay face down.)
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the Mad Party attack.
Flavor-Text: It’s highly skilled at tap-dancing. It waves its cane of ice in time with its graceful movements.

Name: Suicune
Effects: Put 2 [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Said to be the embodiment of north winds, it can instantly purify filthy, murky water.

Name: Galarian Darumaka
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It lived in snowy areas for so long that its fire sac cooled off and atrophied. It now has an organ that generates cold instead.

Name: Galarian Darmanitan
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Crushing Headbutt.
Flavor-Text: On days when blizzards blow through, it comes down to where people live. It stashes food in the snowball on its head, taking it home for later.

Name: Sobble
Flavor-Text: When scared, this Pokémon cries. Its tears pack the chemical punch of 100 onions, and attackers won’t be able to resist weeping.

Name: Drizzile
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may search your deck for a Trainer card, reveal it, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: A clever combatant, this Pokémon battles using water balloons created with moisture secreted from its palms.

Name: Inteleon
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 Trainer cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It has many hidden capabilities, such as fingertips that can shoot water and a membrane on its back that it can use to glide through the air.

Name: Chewtle
Flavor-Text: It starts off battles by attacking with its rock-hard horn, but as soon as the opponent flinches, this Pokémon bites down and never lets go.

Name: Drednaw
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon rapidly extends its retractable neck to sink its sharp fangs into distant enemies and take them down.

Name: Cramorant
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon.
This attack does 20 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s so strong that it can knock out some opponents in a single hit, but it also may forget what it’s battling midfight.

Name: Arrokuda
Flavor-Text: If it sees any movement around it, this Pokémon charges for it straightaway, leading with its sharply pointed jaw. It’s very proud of that jaw.

Name: Barraskewda
Effects: Discard 2 [W] Energy cards from your hand. If you don’t, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has a jaw that’s as sharp as a spear and as strong as steel. Apparently Barraskewda’s flesh is surprisingly tasty, too.

Name: Snom
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It spits out thread imbued with a frigid sort of energy and uses it to tie its body to branches, disguising itself as an icicle while it sleeps.

Name: Frosmoth
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a [W] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched [W] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It shows no mercy to any who desecrate fields and mountains. It will fly around on its icy wings, causing a blizzard to chase offenders away.

Name: Eiscue
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has full HP, it takes 60 less damage from your opponent’s attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It drifted in on the flow of ocean waters from a frigid place. It keeps its head iced constantly to make sure it stays nice and cold.

Name: Dracovish
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can’t play any Pokémon from their hand to evolve their Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Powerful legs and jaws made it the apex predator of its time. Its own overhunting of its prey was what drove it to extinction.

Name: Arctovish
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 60 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Though it’s able to capture prey by freezing its surroundings, it has trouble eating the prey afterward because its mouth is on top of its head.

Name: Rotom
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 Item cards that have the word “Rotom” in their name, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: One boy’s invention led to the development of many different machines that take advantage of Rotom’s unique capabilities.

Name: Yamper
Effects: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is very popular as a herding dog in the Galar region. As it runs, it generates electricity from the base of its tail.

Name: Boltund
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V or Pokémon-GX, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon generates electricity and channels it into its legs to keep them going strong. Boltund can run nonstop for three full days.

Name: Toxel
Flavor-Text: It stores poison in an internal poison sac and secretes that poison through its skin. If you touch this Pokémon, a tingling sensation follows.

Name: Toxtricity
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard the top 5 cards of your opponent’s deck. If tails, discard the top 5 cards of your deck.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: When this Pokémon sounds as if it’s strumming a guitar, it’s actually clawing at the protrusions on its chest to generate electricity.

Name: Pincurchin
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads. If at least 2 of them are heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It feeds on seaweed, using its teeth to scrape it off rocks. Electric current flows from the tips of its spines.

Name: Morpeko
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks. During your opponent’s next turn, that Pokémon can’t use that attack.
This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: As it eats the seeds stored up in its pocket-like pouches, this Pokémon is not just satisfying its constant hunger. It’s also generating electricity.

Name: Dracozolt
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Amping Up attack does 90 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: In ancient times, it was unbeatable thanks to its powerful lower body, but it went extinct anyway after it depleted all its plant-based food sources.

Name: Arctozolt
Ability-Effect: Whenever your opponent attaches an Energy card from their hand to 1 of their Pokémon, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The shaking of its freezing upper half is what generates its electricity. It has a hard time walking around.

Name: Galarian Ponyta
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon will look into your eyes and read the contents of your heart. If it finds evil there, it promptly hides away.

Name: Galarian Rapidash
Ability-Effect: Your Pokémon recover from all Special Conditions and can’t be affected by any Special Conditions.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Brave and prideful, this Pokémon dashes airily through the forest, its steps aided by the psychic power stored in the fur on its fetlocks.

Name: Galarian Corsola
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: Watch your step when wandering areas oceans once covered. What looks like a stone could be this Pokémon, and it will curse you if you kick it.

Name: Galarian Cursola
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack, flip a coin. If heads, the Attacking Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: Its shell is overflowing with its heightened otherworldly energy. The ectoplasm serves as protection for this Pokémon’s core spirit.

Name: Dedenne
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the Mad Party attack.
Flavor-Text: Its upper whiskers are sensors that survey its surroundings. Its lower whiskers are organs that shoot electricity.

Name: Sinistea
Effects: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is said to have been born when a lonely spirit possessed a cold, leftover cup of tea.

Name: Polteageist
Ability-Effect: You must discard a Pokémon that has the Mad Party attack from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 2 cards.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the Mad Party attack.
Flavor-Text: This species lives in antique teapots. Most pots are forgeries, but on rare occasions, an authentic work is found.

Name: Hatenna
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Via the protrusion on its head, it senses other creatures’ emotions. If you don’t have a calm disposition, it will never warm up to you.

Name: Hattrem
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: No matter who you are, if you bring strong emotions near this Pokémon, it will silence you violently.

Name: Hatterene
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Each player discards a card from their hand. (Your opponent discards first.)
Effects: Put 1 damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon for each Pokémon in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: It emits psychic power strong enough to cause headaches as a deterrent to the approach of others.

Name: Milcery
Effects: Heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon was born from sweet-smelling particles in the air. Its body is made of cream.

Name: Alcremie
Effects: Attach any number of basic Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it trusts a Trainer, it will treat them to berries it’s decorated with cream.

Name: Indeedee
Effects: Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: These intelligent Pokémon touch horns with each other to share information between them.

Name: Dreepy
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: After being reborn as a ghost Pokémon, Dreepy wanders the areas it used to inhabit back when it was alive in prehistoric seas.

Name: Drakloak
Effects: Search your deck for a Dreepy and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It’s capable of flying faster than 120 mph. It battles alongside Dreepy and dotes on them until they successfully evolve.

Name: Dragapult
Ability-Effect: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent’s Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: When it isn’t battling, it keeps Dreepy in the holes on its horns. Once a fight starts, it launches the Dreepy like supersonic missiles.

Name: Galarian Farfetch’d
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The Farfetch’d of the Galar region are brave warriors, and they wield thick, tough leeks in battle.

Name: Galarian Sirfetch’d
Effects: This Pokémon can’t use Meteor Assault again until it leaves the Active Spot.
Flavor-Text: Only Farfetch’d that have survived many battles can attain this evolution. When this Pokémon’s leek withers, it will retire from combat.

Name: Galarian Yamask
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: A clay slab with cursed engravings took possession of a Yamask. The slab is said to be absorbing the Yamask’s dark power.

Name: Galarian Runerigus
Effects: For each damage counter on this Galarian Runerigus, put 2 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: A powerful curse was woven into an ancient painting. After absorbing the spirit of a Yamask, the painting began to move.

Name: Rolycoly
Flavor-Text: Most of its body has the same composition as coal. Fittingly, this Pokémon was first discovered in coal mines about 400 years ago.

Name: Carkol
Flavor-Text: It forms coal inside its body. Coal dropped by this Pokémon once helped fuel the lives of people in the Galar region.

Name: Coalossal
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a [R] Energy card, a [F] Energy card, or 1 of each from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: It’s usually peaceful, but the vandalism of mines enrages it. Offenders will be incinerated with flames that reach 2,700 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Silicobra
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn’t happen.
Flavor-Text: It spews sand from its nostrils. While the enemy is blinded, it burrows into the ground to hide.

Name: Sandaconda
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s attacks do 120 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Its unique style of coiling allows it to blast sand out of its sand sac more efficiently.

Name: Clobbopus
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It’s very curious, but its means of investigating things is to try to punch them with its tentacles. The search for food is what brings it onto land.

Name: Grapploct
Effects: Until this Grapploct leaves the Active Spot, the Defending Pokémon’s attacks cost [C][C] more, and the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat. This effect can’t be applied more than once.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: A body made up of nothing but muscle makes the grappling moves this Pokémon performs with its tentacles tremendously powerful.

Name: Falinks
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
This attack does 30 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has “Falinks” in its name.
Flavor-Text: Five of them are troopers, and one is the brass. The brass’s orders are absolute.

Name: Stonjourner
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra Energy attached (in addition to this attack’s cost), this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It stands in grasslands, watching the sun’s descent from zenith to horizon. This Pokémon has a talent for delivering dynamic kicks.

Name: Koffing
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its body is full of poisonous gas. It floats into garbage dumps, seeking out the fumes of raw, rotting trash.

Name: Galarian Weezing
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent’s Pokémon in play have no Abilities, except for Neutralizing Gas.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 4 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon during Pokémon Checkup.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon consumes particles that contaminate the air. Instead of leaving droppings, it expels clean air.

Name: Galarian Zigzagoon
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Thought to be the oldest form of Zigzagoon, it moves in zigzags and wreaks havoc upon its surroundings.

Name: Galarian Linoone
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This very aggressive Pokémon will recklessly challenge opponents stronger than itself.

Name: Galarian Obstagoon
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may put 3 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It evolved after experiencing numerous fights. While crossing its arms, it lets out a shout that would make any opponent flinch.

Name: Nickit
Effects: Your opponent shuffles their hand and puts it on the bottom of their deck. If they put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, they draw 3 cards.
Flavor-Text: Aided by the soft pads on its feet, it silently raids the food stores of other Pokémon. It survives off its ill-gotten gains.

Name: Thievul
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a card you find there and put it on the bottom of their deck.
Flavor-Text: It secretly marks potential targets with a scent. By following the scent, it stalks its targets and steals from them when they least expect it.

Name: Impidimp
Flavor-Text: Through its nose, it sucks in the emanations produced by people and Pokémon when they feel annoyed. It thrives off this negative energy.

Name: Morgrem
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it gets down on all fours as if to beg for forgiveness, it’s trying to lure opponents in so that it can stab them with its spear-like hair.

Name: Grimmsnarl
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks cost [C] more.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: With the hair wrapped around its body helping to enhance its muscles, this Pokémon can overwhelm even Machamp.

Name: Galarian Meowth
Ability-Effect: You must discard 2 cards from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Galarian Perrserker, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Living with a savage, seafaring people has toughened this Pokémon’s body so much that parts of it have turned to iron.

Name: Galarian Perrserker
Ability-Effect: Your [M] Pokémon’s attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: What appears to be an iron helmet is actually hardened hair. This Pokémon lives for the thrill of battle.

Name: Galarian Stunfisk
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if it is Knocked Out), discard an Energy from the Attacking Pokémon.
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Living in mud with a high iron content has given it a strong steel body.

Name: Corviknight
Effects: You may discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, during your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 100 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon reigns supreme in the skies of the Galar region. The black luster of its steel body could drive terror into the heart of any foe.

Name: Cufant
Flavor-Text: It digs up the ground with its trunk. It’s also very strong, being able to carry loads of over five tons without any problem at all.

Name: Copperajah
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can’t be affected by any Special Conditions.
Effects: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They came over from another region long ago and worked together with humans. Their green skin is resistant to water.

Name: Duraludon
Effects: Attach a [M] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its body resembles polished metal, and it’s both lightweight and strong. The only drawback is that it rusts easily.

Name: Minccino
Effects: Look at the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: The way it brushes away grime with its tail can be helpful when cleaning. But its focus on spotlessness can make cleaning more of a hassle.

Name: Cinccino
Ability-Effect: You must discard a card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 2 cards.
Effects: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its body secretes oil that this Pokémon spreads over its nest as a coating to protect it from dust. Cinccino won’t tolerate even a speck of the stuff.

Name: Ducklett
Flavor-Text: They are better at swimming than flying, and they happily eat their favorite food, peat moss, as they dive underwater.

Name: Swanna
Ability-Effect: If you played Bird Keeper from your hand during this turn, ignore all Energy in this Pokémon’s attack costs.
Effects: You may discard a card from your hand. If you do, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Swanna start to dance at dusk. The one dancing in the middle is the leader of the flock.

Name: Bunnelby
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the Mad Party attack.
Flavor-Text: It excels at digging holes. Using its ears, it can dig a nest 33 feet deep in one night.

Name: Oranguru
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may switch a card from your hand with the top card of your deck.
Flavor-Text: It knows the forest inside and out. If it comes across a wounded Pokémon, Oranguru will gather medicinal herbs to treat it.

Name: Skwovet
Flavor-Text: Found throughout the Galar region, this Pokémon becomes uneasy if its cheeks are ever completely empty of berries.

Name: Greedent
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Pokémon Tool card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It stashes berries in its tail—so many berries that they fall out constantly. But this Pokémon is a bit slow-witted, so it doesn’t notice the loss.

Name: Rookidee
Flavor-Text: It will bravely challenge any opponent, no matter how powerful. This Pokémon benefits from every battle—even a defeat increases its strength a bit.

Name: Corvisquire
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Smart enough to use tools in battle, these Pokémon have been seen picking up rocks and flinging them or using ropes to wrap up enemies.

Name: Wooloo
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 10 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: If its fleece grows too long, Wooloo won’t be able to move. Cloth made with the wool of this Pokémon is surprisingly strong.

Name: Dubwool
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its majestic horns are meant only to impress the opposite gender. They never see use in battle.

Name: Rillaboom V
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic [G] Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Rillaboom VMAX
Effects: You may discard up to 3 [G] Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 50 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Charizard VMAX
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Centiskorch V
Effects: You may discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Centiskorch VMAX
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you did any damage with this attack, you may attach a [R] Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Lapras V
Effects: Attach a [W] Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon. If you do, switch it with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Put 2 [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Lapras VMAX
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Toxtricity V
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 90 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Toxtricity VMAX
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 80 more damage.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Indeedee V
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 60 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Falinks V
Ability-Effect: All of your Pokémon that have “Falinks” in their name take 20 less damage from your opponent’s attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Grimmsnarl V
Effects: Put 2 [D] Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Grimmsnarl VMAX
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each extra [D] Energy attached to this Pokémon (in addition to this attack’s cost). You can’t add more than 100 damage in this way.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Ditto V
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may choose a Basic Pokémon V from your discard pile and switch it with this Pokémon. Any attached cards, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.
Effects: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Ditto VMAX
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks and use it as this attack.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Dubwool V
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Eternatus V
Effects: You may attach a [D] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon VMAX, this attack does 120 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Eternatus VMAX
Ability-Effect: If all of your Pokémon in play are [D] type, you can have up to 8 Pokémon on your Bench, and you can’t put non-[D] Pokémon into play. (If this Ability stops working, discard Pokémon from your Bench until you have 5.)
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each of your [D] Pokémon in play.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.
